{{Quote|The School follows its own will… but someone must ever be the first to swim…|Primor|<ref name="marvel74" />}}
'''Primor''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Inleshat/Legends|Inleshat]] who was the leader, or "The First to Swim," of the [[Iskalonian]] [[The School|School]] on [[Iskalon/Legends|Iskalon]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. A calm leader who prided himself on knowing his people's inclinations, Primor presided over the School during the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] brutal subjugation of the [[Iskalon system/Legends|Iskalon system]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]—although Primor had [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] leanings, the Empire's war crimes on nearby [[Telfrey]] and [[Gamandar]] cowed him into submission. Nevertheless Primor reluctantly sanctioned a Rebel operation on Gamandar in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], only to [[Death/Legends|die]] when Imperial forces triggered a massive tidal wave on Iskalon known as the [[Iskalon Effect]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|You have always been the least political of creatures... the least concerned with the good of the School! Don't you see that there is danger? Rebellion is not some game the fry play—!|Primor, to Mone|<ref name="marvel74" />}}
[[File:IskalonIII.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Primor surrounded by several members of the School.]]
A [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Inleshat/Legends|Inleshat]]—an aquatic [[Species/Legends|species]] that formed part of the [[Iskalonian]] [[The School|School]] on the [[Water/Legends|water]]-covered [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Iskalon/Legends|Iskalon]]—<ref name="gamer1" />Primor became the School's leader,<ref name="marvel74" /> or "The First to Swim,"<ref name="gamer1" /> by the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Primor had a son, [[Mone]], who was full-grown and [[Marriage/Legends|married]] to a female named [[Kendle]] by [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], by which time the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] had established a presence in the [[Iskalon system/Legends|Iskalon system]], after [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] [[Tay Vanis]] and [[Yom Argo]], natives of nearby [[Telfrey]], returned to their home system to foment rebellious sentiment. The Empire responded by [[Decimation of Telfrey|rendering Telfrey uninhabitable]], brutally subjugating neighboring [[Gamandar]] and occupying Iskalon itself, forcing Primor to accept a [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] presence on his world.<ref name="marvel74" />

Argo and Vanis disappeared soon after, leading a Rebel team—led by [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], an old acquaintance of Primor's—to travel to Iskalon to investigate. Although they had previously learned Argo had perished in a crash on [[Lahsbane/Legends|Lahsbane]], they held hope that Vanis could still be alive. The Empire's tactics had instilled a deep fear in Primor, who attempted to convince Calrissian and his friends not to travel to Gamandar to attempt to find Vanis, but he reluctantly saw them off when Mone insisted he would travel with them. With Calrissian having only taken Mone and the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] with him, Primor instead occupied his time with introducing their other two [[Human/Legends|Human]] companions, Rebel leaders [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], to the School's underwater way of life.<ref name="marvel74" /> However, unbeknownst to Primor, his daughter-in-law Kendle was an Imperial collaborator—<ref name="marvel75">[[Star Wars (1977) 75|''Star Wars'' (1977) 75]]</ref> after she informed Imperial [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Griggor Tower]] that Rebel agents were en route to Gamandar, he fired a [[Missile/Legends|missile]] into the Iskalon seas and triggered the [[Iskalon Effect]], a massive tidal wave that devastated the planet and killed Primor.<ref name="marvel75" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|My people follow me because I never try to take them against the drift of their inclinations...|Primor|<ref name="marvel74" />}}
A friendly man welcoming to outsiders, Primor took joy in showing the wonders of Iskalon to visitors, and introducing them to the School's lifestyle. Although Primor was the leader of the School, he bucked against any aspersions that he was a monarch—to him, his leadership came from being in tune with the wants and desires of the people, not from his title. Primor supported the Rebellion, but the Empire's incredibly violent subjugation of the Iskalon system succeeded in cowing the Inleshat, who feared to go against their occupiers. Most of all, he feared for his son Mone, whom he saw as a foolish pleasure-seeker without a real understanding of the consequences of rebellion.<ref name="marvel74" />

==Behind the scenes==
Primor first appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 74|''Star Wars'' (1977) 74]], written by [[Mary Jo Duffy]], illustrated by [[Ron Frenz]] and released in [[1983]].<ref name="marvel74" />
